section.heading: Receipt of Organization communications by Secretary of State

subsection.heading: Concurrence with Secretaries of Commerce and the Interior on regulatory measures; action on other matters

paragraph.content: receive, on behalf of the United States, reports, requests, recommendations, proposals, and other communications of the Organization and its subsidiary organs;
with the concurrence of the Secretary of Commerce and the Secretary of the Interior, approve, object to, or withdraw objections to regulatory measures proposed in accordance with the Convention; and
act upon, or refer to other appropriate authority, any communication referred to in paragraph (1) of this subsection other than a proposed regulatory measure.

subsection.heading: Submission to President on failure of concurrence on regulatory matters
subsection.chapeau: If the concurrence required under subsection (a)(2) of this section has not been obtained by the Secretary of State—

paragraph.content: regarding the approval of, or the objection to, a proposed regulatory measure within forty-five days after the measure was received on behalf of the United States; or
regarding the withdrawal of an objection of the United States to a proposed regulatory measure within forty-five days after such withdrawal is proposed by the Secretary of State;

subsection.continuation: the Secretary of State shall submit the matter in disagreement, together with a statement of the opposing positions, to the President for timely disposition.
